title: Campaign Monitor vs Constant Contact (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Campaign Monitor vs Constant Contact: compare pricing, features, and real use cases to find the right email tool for your needs.
h1: Campaign Monitor vs Constant Contact: Side-by-Side Comparison (2025)
intro: Choosing between Campaign Monitor and Constant Contact comes down to your specific use case. Both are legitimate email marketing platforms, but they serve different audiences. Campaign Monitor is built for agencies and teams managing multiple brands, while Constant Contact targets nonprofits and local businesses. This comparison covers the key differences so you can decide quickly.

comparison_table.tool_a: Agencies And Teams Managing Multiple Brands

choose_a_if: You are primarily focused on agencies and teams managing multiple brands
choose_b_if: You are primarily focused on nonprofits and local businesses

faq.q: Is Campaign Monitor better than Constant Contact?
faq.a: It depends on your use case. Campaign Monitor wins for agencies and teams managing multiple brands, while Constant Contact is stronger for nonprofits and local businesses. Neither is objectively better — the right choice depends on your specific needs.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Campaign Monitor or Constant Contact?
faq.a: Campaign Monitor starts at a lower price point ($11/mo vs $12/mo). However, pricing scales with subscriber count, so the total cost depends on your list size.

faq.q: Can I switch from Constant Contact to Campaign Monitor?
faq.a: Yes, switching from Constant Contact to Campaign Monitor is possible. Most platforms support CSV import/export for subscriber lists. Plan for some time to recreate automations and templates, as these rarely transfer directly between platforms.
